zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan De Sydenham於1377年出生在英國英格蘭多瑟特時，她的父親Richard Sydenham40歲，母親Joan Dallingridge43歲。她和Thomas Bratton至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1408年在英國英格蘭戴文享年31歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sydenham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Sydenham in Bridgwater (Somerset), Sydenham Damerel (Devon), and Sydenham (Oxon).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan De Sydenham was born in 1377, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Sydenham, was 40 and her mother, Joan Dallingridge, was 43. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Thomas Bratton. She died in 1408, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 31.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan De Sydenham est née en 1377, à Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Sydenham, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Joan Dallingridge, avait 43 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Thomas Bratton. Elle est décédée en 1408, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 31 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan De Sydenham 1377 in Dorset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Sydenham 40 und ihre Mutter Joan Dallingridge 43. Sie hatte mit Thomas Bratton mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1408 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De Sydenham è nata il nel 1377 nel Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard Sydenham, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Joan Dallingridge, aveva 43 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Thomas Bratton. Morì nel 1408, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 31 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan De Sydenham(이)가 1377년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Sydenham(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Dallingridge(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Bratton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1408년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.
